current_fact.fact_text: Back in 1801, British troops under Sir Ralph Abercromby fought the Battle of Abukir as they landed in Egypt, aiming to push out Napoleon’s forces. The clash unfolded on sands where empires imagined they could redraw entire regions.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

current_fact.match_reason: A colonial battle on Egyptian shores is juxtaposed with a peaceful, contemporary journey through medinas and Afro-Arab soundscapes reshaping the same geography.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1702, Anne, the younger sister of Mary II, became queen regnant of England, Scotland, and Ireland. Her reign would quietly oversee the 1707 Act of Union, stitching separate crowns into a new political fabric.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

queue.match_reason: A new queen ascending to the throne pairs playfully with an episode titled "Queen of Spades," both exploring power, vulnerability, and the stories we attach to queens.

queue.fact_text: In 1988, Aeroflot Flight 3739 was hijacked midair by members of the Ovechkin family, who were desperate to escape the Soviet Union. The incident turned a routine domestic flight into a violent, chaotic microcosm of late-Soviet tension and longing to flee.

episode.title: Morning Drops #32 - A Weekend In London
episode.description: Recorded in London, marking a weekend reunion between Gaia and Yasmin. A selection inspired by London's energy, seen through the eyes of two inseparable friends — music from concerts, club nights, and long walks across town. Four tracks to ease you into the week at the right pace.
songs: Jimmy Castor Bunch - It's Just Begun
The Asphodells - Late Flowering Lust (Phil Kieran Remix)

queue.match_reason: A desperate attempt to break out of a closed system is contrasted with an episode about free movement across a global city, where borders feel porous and possibility open.

queue.fact_text: Also in 1979, Philips demonstrated the compact disc to the public for the first time, spinning a small reflective disc that could hold hours of crystal-clear audio. That demo quietly announced the digital future of how humans would listen, collect, and obsess over music.

episode.title: Morning Drops #07 - secret_mp3_player_1
episode.description: Inspired by a throwback MP3 player on Instagram, Yasmin and Gaia take you on a nostalgic journey to the early 2000s. LimeWire, eMule, guilty-pleasure playlists — remember those? Hit play and relive the songs that shaped a generation.

queue.match_reason: The birth of the CD, a key step toward digital music, pairs with an episode celebrating the MP3 era — two chapters in the same story of how we carry songs in our pockets.

queue.fact_text: On March 8, 1917, women workers and activists in Petrograd took to the streets for International Women's Day, sparking protests that would swell into the February Revolution. Their march helped topple an empire and rewrote the course of Russian history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: The original International Women’s Day protests that ignited revolution are matched with an episode explicitly dedicated to Women's Day and powerful female voices.

queue.fact_text: A century later in 2018, Pakistan saw its first Aurat March on International Women’s Day, as women and allies filled Karachi’s streets demanding bodily autonomy and an end to gender-based violence. The slogan “Mera Jism Meri Marzi” became a rallying cry that challenged deep-rooted norms.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

queue.match_reason: A modern feminist march with a bold slogan meets an episode celebrating unapologetically powerful women in music and their voices of resistance.

queue.fact_text: On this date in 1965, Aeroflot Flight 513 crashed during takeoff from Kuybyshev Airport, killing 30 of the 39 people onboard. It was one of several Soviet-era incidents that quietly pushed aviation toward stricter safety standards and design reforms.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful

queue.match_reason: A somber aviation disaster is paired with a reflective, hopeful episode about enduring difficult chapters and moving forward after turbulence.

queue.fact_text: In 1949, French President Vincent Auriol and former emperor Bảo Đại signed the Élysée Accords, creating the State of Vietnam as a French-backed alternative to Hồ Chí Minh’s revolutionary government. It was an attempt to manage decolonization by redesigning sovereignty on paper.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

queue.match_reason: A political attempt to redraw borders and identities is contrasted with a personal story of crossing borders by choice, exploring how places shape identity from the ground up.

queue.fact_text: On March 8, 1937, the Battle of Guadalajara began during the Spanish Civil War, as Republican and fascist forces clashed on muddy roads near Madrid. International volunteers and experimental tactics turned the battle into a grim rehearsal for the wider conflicts to come.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: A pivotal Spanish Civil War battle finds its cultural counterpoint in an episode about Flamenco, another intense Spanish expression shaped by struggle and history.

queue.fact_text: In 1936, the Daytona Beach and Road Course hosted its first oval stock car race, sending drivers roaring along packed sand at the ocean’s edge. Those improvised beach circuits would eventually evolve into the epic speedways of NASCAR lore.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: Early stock car races on sand, all speed and motion, connect neatly with an episode designed for being "on the go" and rolling down long roads.

queue.fact_text: On March 8, 1979, the same year that Io’s volcanoes were revealed, another kind of eruption was quietly brewing in living rooms: the first public demo of the compact disc by Philips. With a laser and a spinning disc, it hinted at a world where music would be copied as precisely as code.

episode.title: Morning Drops #47 - The Rise Of Autotune
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the explosive rise of Auto-Tune, a tool that quietly changed the sound of music without asking for attention. From fragile, imperfect recordings to voices shaped with precision, this episode follows the moment when correction became standard and sound became controlled.

Along the way, we discover the curious story of how it was invented, how it moved from the background of studios to the center of debate, and how a few artists turned it into something expressive rather than hidden.

Listen slowly, and let us know what you think about Autotune.
songs: Chet Baker – I Fall in Love Too Easily

queue.match_reason: A technological leap that digitised sound is matched with an episode about another controversial tool that reshaped how recorded music is engineered and heard.

queue.fact_text: When the first International Women's Day protests in Petrograd lit the fuse for revolution in 1917, they unknowingly started a tradition that would echo across continents. Over a century later, marches like Pakistan’s Aurat March and countless others still trace their lineage back to that cold Russian spring.

episode.title: Morning Drops #37 - Happy Birthday Gaia
episode.description: A special Morning Drop to celebrate Gaia's birthday and the first anniversary of Sedna FM. A journey through friendship, music, and voice messages from the people who've known her longest. From street dance classes in Milan to creative adventures in London, a tribute to growing up together and showing up for each other.
songs: The Mighty Tiny & The Many Few - Good Foot

queue.match_reason: A global day born from protest is paired with a celebratory episode that opens with "Woman" and honors the stories and connections that women build over time.

queue.fact_text: As Daytona’s racers tore along the sand in 1936, few imagined that stock car racing would become a cultural phenomenon with its own myths, heroes, and soundtrack. The roar of those early engines was the first verse in a long, loud song about speed and spectacle.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: Engines, adrenaline, and highways meet an episode full of electric guitars and road-ready rock energy.

queue.fact_text: Across all these March 8ths, from volcanoes on Io to women marching in Petrograd and Karachi, one thread stands out: the way small moments — a protest, a demo, a landing, a crash — ripple outward into new worlds. History on this date is less a line than a constellation of turning points.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A reflective summary of March 8 as a day of journeys and turning points fits an episode framed as a collective voyage through sound and new territories.

published.fact_text: In 1979, Voyager 1 quietly transformed our idea of what a moon could be when it sent back images of Io spewing plumes of molten rock into space. Until then, active volcanism beyond Earth was just a theory; Io’s raging sulfurous eruptions proved that distant worlds can be violently alive.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A discovery that turned "empty" outer space into a volcanically active neighborhood pairs with an episode about reimagining emptiness as creative space.

published.fact_text: On March 8, 2001, Space Shuttle Discovery launched the STS-102 mission, ferrying the Expedition 2 crew to the International Space Station for a long-duration stay. It was part of the early, fragile phase of building a permanently inhabited outpost in orbit.

episode.title: Morning Drops #50 - Postcards from Earth-London
episode.description:
Postcards from Earth-London is a Morning Drops walk through the city Gaia has called home for the past ten years, a place made of sound, memory, and late-night streets.
Four tracks guide the journey: from iconic landmarks to quieter corners, from romance to reality checks, and back to the reason the city still feels alive: music.

published.match_reason: A mission connecting Earth and orbit joins an episode framed as "postcards from Earth," including the fitting track "Learning to Fly."

published.fact_text: In 1844, Iceland reopened the Althing, its ancient parliament that traces its roots back to the Viking Age. After 45 years of silence, one of the world's oldest legislative assemblies began to speak for the island again.

episode.title: Morning Drops #28 - Under Scottish Skies
episode.description: Gaia shares a personal moment of transition — a shift in seasons, in rhythm, and in life. From this space of quiet change comes a musical journey through Scotland: not the usual names, but voices that speak from the edges. A personal ritual and a small act of curiosity: an invitation to notice what grounds us when everything moves.
songs: Paolo Nutini - Pencil Full of Lead

published.match_reason: An old northern parliament reawakening is paired with an episode steeped in Celtic landscapes and questions of what grounds a people.

published.fact_text: On a stormy day in 2017, the Azure Window — a vast limestone arch on the Maltese island of Gozo — collapsed into the sea. A natural icon that had stood for centuries vanished in hours, a reminder of how quickly coastal landscapes can be rewritten.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

published.match_reason: A vanished Mediterranean rock arch sails alongside a sunlit musical voyage across the same sea and its coastal cultures.

published.fact_text: In 2010, TNA Wrestling shifted its flagship show, TNA Impact!, to Monday nights in an audacious attempt to challenge WWE’s long-held television dominance. The experiment ultimately failed, but it became a curious chapter in the ongoing battle to reinvent televised wrestling.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

published.match_reason: A night of high-drama combat entertainment links naturally to an episode born from real combat sports training and the rhythm of fighting arts.

published.fact_text: On March 8, 1965, U.S. Marines waded ashore at Da Nang, marking the first large-scale deployment of American ground combat troops in the Vietnam War. It was a turning point that shifted the conflict from an advisory role into full-scale U.S. military involvement.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: A key escalation in a controversial war resonates with an episode centered on music that explicitly confronts militarism and political power.

published.fact_text: In 1983, at a gathering of evangelical leaders, U.S. President Ronald Reagan branded the Soviet Union an “evil empire.” The phrase crystallized the moral framing of the late Cold War and echoed through political rhetoric and popular culture for decades.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: A speech that polarised an entire planet pairs with an episode questioning how modern networks and technologies quietly shape our perceptions of ‘us’ and ‘them’.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1942, Dutch colonial forces in the East Indies surrendered Java to the advancing Imperial Japanese Army. The fall of the island accelerated the unraveling of European colonial rule in Southeast Asia.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

published.match_reason: A wartime shift that foreshadowed decolonization is matched with a musical history rooted in postcolonial assertion of identity and freedom.

published.fact_text: Also in 1942, Japanese forces captured Rangoon in Burma, seizing a crucial port and cutting the last major Allied land route into China. The city’s fall turned jungle terrain and mountain passes into lifelines for resistance and supply.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

published.match_reason: A campaign fought over supply routes and occupied homelands resonates with desert-blues stories of movement, occupation, exile, and survival.

published.fact_text: In 1962, a Turkish Airlines Fokker F27 known as the "Friendship" crashed into Mount Medetsiz in Turkey’s Taurus Mountains, killing all 11 people on board. The accident highlighted how quickly routine regional flights can intersect with unforgiving terrain.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: A tragic brush between machine and mountain is paired with a calm, cinematic journey into extreme environments, reflecting on risk, nature, and fragile human presence.

published.fact_text: Back in 1801, British troops under Sir Ralph Abercromby fought the Battle of Abukir as they landed in Egypt, aiming to push out Napoleon’s forces. The clash unfolded on sands where empires imagined they could redraw entire regions.

episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: A colonial battle on Egyptian shores is juxtaposed with a peaceful, contemporary journey through medinas and Afro-Arab soundscapes reshaping the same geography.
